title: South Africa Ordinary Passport Application (Form BI-73)
description: Apply for an ordinary South African passport as an adult (16 years or older) applying for the first time, in person at a Department of Home Affairs office within South Africa, using Form BI-73 ('Application for a South African Passport or Travel Document'). It does not submit the application; the live dha.gov.za source and the applicant's local Home Affairs office are always authoritative (see VERIFICATION.md). This document models the domestic, ordinary/permanent 32-page passport pathway specifically; Form BI-73 also covers Maxi, Diplomatic, Official, Crew Member, Temporary, and Emergency passports/travel documents (each with its own supporting-document and fee requirements not modeled here) and a Child Passport/Endorsement pathway requiring parental consent (Section C of the form), out of scope for this first-time-adult document. Applying from outside South Africa, at a South African mission/consulate abroad, is a separate process with different fees, out of scope here.

authority.name: Department of Home Affairs, Republic of South Africa

verification.notes: First reference schema for South Africa (a new jurisdiction for this registry). Form BI-73 fields (Sections A, D, and E) were read directly from the official dha.gov.za-hosted PDF cited in `source.url` (confirmed live and retrievable). That government-hosted copy is a large, low-contrast scanned image; its field layout was cross-checked, page by page, against a legible full-resolution mirror of the identical current BI-73/BI-529 form pair distributed by the South African Embassy in Washington, D.C. (saembassy.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/DHA-73-1.pdf) and against that embassy's separate consular requirements checklist (suedafrika.org/downloads/BI-73_Passport.pdf), both of which show the same 'G.P.S. 017-9750' print code and identical section structure as the government original. Section C (parental consent for a minor) is out of scope for this first-time-adult document. The domestic 32-page ordinary-passport fee (ZAR 600) is sourced from the Department of Home Affairs' own fee-increase announcement on the government's gov.za media-statements page, effective 2026-11-01 [sic: 2022-11-01] and confirmed still current by multiple independent 2026 secondary sources; it is distinct from and lower than the fee charged at foreign missions. Two items are flagged as not independently confirmed for the domestic (non-consular) pathway specifically: (1) whether Form BI-529 (Determination of Citizenship Status) is required for every domestic first-time applicant or only those with a citizenship-status question (the consular checklist that names it as an unconditional requirement is for an abroad/mission submission, which inherently raises citizenship questions the BI-73 form itself gates behind its Section D questions) — modeled here as `requiredWhen` the applicant answers Section D's citizenship questions in a way that raises a citizenship-determination need, pending direct confirmation from a domestic Home Affairs office; (2) the exact DHA-9 fingerprint-collection procedure, sourced only from the same consular checklist as a secondary source, since it is a biometric process rather than a fillable form. Status remains 'draft' pending a full field-by-field comparison against a live domestic Home Affairs office visit or an authoritative first-party domestic checklist.

fields.name: applicationType
fields.label: Application for (mark which is applicable)
fields.description: The type of document requested on Form BI-73. This schema's required-document set and fee model the ordinary_passport pathway; the other values are captured because they appear as tick-boxes on the same form, but their own supporting-document and fee requirements are out of scope here.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — 'APPLICATION FOR (mark which is applicable with an X)'

fields.name: deliverToPostalAddress
fields.label: Must the document be forwarded to my address indicated below?
fields.description: If false, the completed passport is instead collected in person from the Home Affairs office where the application was lodged.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — 'The document must be forwarded to my address indicated below: YES/NO'

fields.name: surname
fields.label: Surname
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Surname'

fields.name: forenamesInFull
fields.label: Forenames in full
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Forenames in full'

fields.name: previousSurname
fields.label: Previous surname(s), if applicable
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Leave blank if the applicant has never used a different surname.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Previous surname(s)'

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Marital status
fields.description: As printed on Form BI-73: unmarried, married, widower, widow, or divorced.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Marital status'

fields.name: gender
fields.label: Gender
fields.description: Form BI-73 offers only Male and Female tick-boxes; unlike some other jurisdictions' passport forms it has no third option.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Gender: Male / Female'

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Full date, YYYY-MM-DD.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Date of birth'

fields.name: identityNumber
fields.label: South African identity number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. The 13-digit South African ID number as printed on the applicant's ID book or smart ID card; a passport can only be issued to an applicant in possession of one (Form BI-73, Instruction 1(e)).
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Identity number'

fields.name: countryOfBirth
fields.label: Country of birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Country of birth'

fields.name: placeOfBirth
fields.label: Place of birth
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Town or city of birth.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Place of birth'

fields.name: postalAddressLines
fields.label: Postal address
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Free-text postal address as it appears on the multi-line 'Postal address' box on the form.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Postal address'

fields.name: postalCode
fields.label: Postal code
fields.description: South African postal codes are 4 digits.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Postal address — Code'

fields.name: homeTelephone
fields.label: Home telephone number and code
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. In international E.164 format, e.g. +27211234567.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Home Tel. No. and code'

fields.name: workTelephone
fields.label: Work telephone number and code
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. In international E.164 format, e.g. +27211234567.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Work Tel. No. and code'

fields.name: cellNumber
fields.label: Cell phone number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. In international E.164 format, e.g. +27821234567. Used by the Home Affairs office to notify the applicant when the passport is ready.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section A, 'Cell No.'

fields.name: acquiredOtherCitizenship
fields.label: Have you acquired citizenship of another country on application?
fields.description: Section D must be completed by all applicants.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section D(a), 'Have you acquired citizenship of another country on application?'

fields.name: otherCitizenshipCountry
fields.label: Other country of citizenship
fields.description: Required when acquiredOtherCitizenship is true.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section D(a) table, 'Country'

fields.name: otherCitizenshipPlaceObtained
fields.label: Place other citizenship was obtained
fields.description: Required when acquiredOtherCitizenship is true.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section D(a) table, 'Place obtained'

fields.name: otherCitizenshipMeans
fields.label: Means of obtaining other citizenship
fields.description: Required when acquiredOtherCitizenship is true, e.g. by marriage, naturalisation, descent, or registration.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section D(a) table, 'Means of obtaining'

fields.name: otherCitizenshipDate
fields.label: Date other citizenship was obtained
fields.description: Required when acquiredOtherCitizenship is true.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section D(a) table, 'Date'

fields.name: previouslyIssuedTravelDocument
fields.label: Have travel facilities previously been issued to you?
fields.description: Section D must be completed by all applicants. South African passports or other travel documents presently in the applicant's possession must be submitted with the application unless lost, stolen, or damaged.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section D(b), 'Have travel facilities previously been issued to you?'

fields.name: previousTravelDocumentDate
fields.label: Date previous travel document was issued
fields.description: Required when previouslyIssuedTravelDocument is true.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section D(b), 'if so, when?'

fields.name: previousTravelDocumentNumber
fields.label: Previous passport / travel document number
fields.description: Personal identifier; handle as sensitive data. Required when previouslyIssuedTravelDocument is true.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section D(b), 'Passport / document number'

fields.name: declarationTruthfulComplete
fields.label: I declare that the information furnished above is to the best of my knowledge and belief correct
fields.description: Must be true. The applicant also concurs that if the passport is not claimed within 3 months of the date of issue, it will be destroyed and the fee forfeited.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section E, Declaration

fields.name: declarationDate
fields.label: Declaration date
fields.description: Date the applicant signs the declaration in Section E.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section E, 'Date'

fields.name: declarationPlace
fields.label: Declaration place
fields.description: Town or city where the applicant signs the declaration in Section E.
fields.sourceRef: Form BI-73 — Section E, 'Place'

documents.label: Notarized copy of South African ID book or smart ID card
documents.sourceRef: Form BI-73, Instruction 1(e): a passport can only be issued to an applicant in possession of an identity number, who must submit identity documents along with the application

documents.label: Full set of fingerprints (Form DHA-9)
documents.sourceRef: Form BI-73, Instruction 1(f): a full set of fingerprints (or thumbprint) is required from all applicants 16 years and older, in addition to the left thumbprint taken on the form itself; can be taken at a police station or by a notary public. This document is a biometric-collection process rather than a fillable form; sourced from a South African consular office's requirements checklist, not independently confirmed for the domestic pathway.

documents.label: Determination of Citizenship Status (Form BI-529)
documents.sourceRef: Form BI-529 header: 'Information to be furnished in full in order to assist in determining your citizenship status.' Whether domestic Home Affairs offices require this unconditionally for every first-time application, or only when a citizenship question arises, is not independently confirmed for the domestic pathway (see VERIFICATION.md).

documents.label: Passport photographs
documents.sourceRef: Form BI-73, Instruction 3(a): two recent, clear, unmounted photographs (head and shoulders) measuring 35mm x 45mm; the certifying officer endorses one and affixes the other to the application form. This document models the printed photographs submitted with the paper form, not a digital upload.

documents.label: Signature of applicant
documents.statement: I, ..., hereby declare that the information furnished above is to the best of my knowledge and belief correct and I concur that if I do not claim my passport within three (3) months from date of issue, my passport will be destroyed and I will forfeit my fee.

documents.label: Ordinary (32-page) passport application fee
documents.sourceRef: Department of Home Affairs fee approval (gov.za media statement, effective 2022-11-01): domestic 32-page adult passport fee is ZAR 600, confirmed current as of 2026. This is the domestic (within-South-Africa) fee; the fee at a foreign mission/consulate is higher (ZAR 1,200) and out of scope here. Form BI-73, Instruction 2(b): fees are payable in cash upon submission of the application at a Home Affairs office.

steps.title: Application type and particulars of applicant
